codeReview.summary: DemoSeeder and DemoUsersSeeder create the required demo data (2 users, 1 ressourcerie in dept 49, 5 products). However, both seeders lack idempotency - they will fail if run a second time due to unique constraint violations. This is a non-blocking issue since the validation assertions (VAL-DEMO-003, VAL-DEMO-004, VAL-DEMO-005) only require the seeders to create the data once, not to be re-runnable.

issues.description: DemoUsersSeeder uses User::create() directly without checking if users already exist. If run twice, will fail with 'UNIQUE constraint failed: users.email' for client@demo.pivot and ressourcier@demo.pivot. Should use firstOrCreate or check existence first for idempotency.
Same idempotency issue for ressourcier@demo.pivot user.
No null check on $ressourcier after User::where()->first(). If DemoUsersSeeder fails, $ressourcier will be null and accessing $ressourcier->id will cause a runtime error.
DemoSeeder creates new ressourcerie and products without checking if they already exist. Re-running will create duplicates. Should check existence first for idempotency.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires running 'php artisan migrate' and 'php artisan db:seed' commands to verify implementation, but PHP is not available in the current environment. The worker spent time investigating PHP availability via nix-shell and docker before acknowledging the limitation. services.yaml doesn't document PHP as a required service.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'Command not found. The command or program may not be installed or not in PATH. bash: ligne 1: php: commande introuvable'. Worker then tried which php8.2, nix-shell, docker before concluding PHP wasn't available.

summary: DemoSeeder implementation meets all validation assertions (VAL-DEMO-003, VAL-DEMO-004, VAL-DEMO-005) by creating client@demo.pivot and ressourcier@demo.pivot users with bcrypt passwords, a ressourcerie in department 49, and 5 sample products. Code follows Laravel conventions (final class, typed methods, proper namespace). Non-blocking issues: seeders lack idempotency and will fail if re-run, but this doesn't affect initial validation.
